title: Subtract — Regroup Across Zeros
essential_question: How do you subtract when the top number has zeros?

hints: Zeros can't give up a unit directly — borrow from the next non-zero place to the left.
Try drawing a picture or using a place-value chart.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 264. After regrouping across zeros, you get 264.
Not quite. The answer is B. Borrow across zeros one place at a time.

hints: Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.
Zeros can't give up a unit directly — borrow from the next non-zero place to the left.
Try drawing a picture or using a place-value chart.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 1766. After regrouping across zeros, you get 1,766.

pretest.question: You can't borrow from a 0 directly; you must first borrow from a nonzero place to the left.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is True.

hints: Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.
Zeros can't give up a unit directly — borrow from the next non-zero place to the left.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 5433. After regrouping across zeros, you get 5,433.

learn.title: The Challenge of Zeros
learn.content: Subtracting numbers like 500 − 236 is tricky because the tens and ones places have zeros. You can't regroup from a zero directly.
learn.tts: Subtracting numbers like 500 − 236 is tricky because the tens and ones places have zeros. You can't regroup from a zero directly.

learn.title: Borrow Across Zeros
learn.content: Look left to find a nonzero digit. Borrow 1 from it, then pass the value through the zeros. 500 becomes 4 hundreds, 9 tens, 10 ones.
learn.tts: Look left to find a nonzero digit. Borrow 1 from it, then pass the value through the zeros. 500 becomes 4 hundreds, 9 tens, 10 ones.

learn.title: Walk Through: 500 − 236
learn.content: Step 1: Change 500 to 4|9|10 (4 hundreds, 9 tens, 10 ones). Step 2: 10−6=4. Step 3: 9−3=6. Step 4: 4−2=2. Answer: 264.
learn.tts: Step 1: Change 500 to 4|9|10 (4 hundreds, 9 tens, 10 ones). Step 2: 10−6=4. Step 3: 9−3=6. Step 4: 4−2=2. Answer: 264.

learn.title: Multiple Zeros
learn.content: Numbers like 10,000 have many zeros. Regrouping spreads the borrowed 1 across every zero: 9|9|9|10.
learn.tts: Numbers like 10,000 have many zeros. Regrouping spreads the borrowed 1 across every zero: 9|9|9|10.

learn.title: Estimate to Check
learn.content: Estimate 1,000 − 457 ≈ 1,000 − 500 = 500. The real answer 543 is close, so it's reasonable.
learn.tts: Estimate 1,000 − 457 ≈ 1,000 − 500 = 500. The real answer 543 is close, so it's reasonable.
